zh.homeIntro1: 珍妮·狄克逊（Jeane Dixon，1904年1月5日—1997年1月25日），美国最著名的自称通灵预言家。她声称通过水晶球和占星术感应未来，在冷战时期的美国社会具有巨大影响力，甚至被传曾为多位美国总统提供建议。
zh.homeIntro2: 狄克逊最广为人知的预言是1956年在《Parade》杂志上声称1960年大选将由一位民主党人赢得，且此人将在任内遭到暗杀或死于任上——这与肯尼迪的遭遇高度吻合。然而，她同样做出了大量完全错误的预言，包括第三次世界大战、苏联率先登月等。
zh.homeIntro3: 数学家约翰·艾伦·保罗斯因此创造了「狄克逊效应」（Jeane Dixon effect）一词：人们倾向于记住少数命中的预言，而忽略大量失败的预测。这个认知偏差概念至今仍被广泛引用。本站收录了她的代表性预言，并进行了逐条验证。
zh.coreQuote: 「1960年的大选将由一位民主党人赢得，而他将在任内遭到暗杀或死于任上。」——1956年《Parade》杂志
keyPointsList: 1956年 —— 在《Parade》杂志发表肯尼迪遇刺相关预言
1960年代 —— 成为全美最知名的通灵人士
1969年 —— 露丝·蒙哥马利出版《预言的礼物》，狄克逊声名达到顶峰
1986年 —— 约翰·艾伦·保罗斯提出「狄克逊效应」概念
sourceList: 预言内容整理自珍妮·狄克逊公开发表的报刊专栏、书籍及传记（Wikipedia）
1956年《Parade》杂志原文预言是核心一手来源（Snopes 考证）
「狄克逊效应」由数学家约翰·艾伦·保罗斯在其1988年著作《Innumeracy》中提出（Wikipedia: Jeane Dixon effect）
验证评价基于公开新闻报道和历史记录
站点图标采用水晶球符号——狄克逊以手持水晶球占卜的形象闻名于世

en.homeIntro1: Jeane Dixon (January 5, 1904 – January 25, 1997) was America's most famous self-proclaimed psychic. She claimed to see the future through a crystal ball and astrology, wielding enormous influence in Cold War-era American society. She was even rumored to have advised multiple US presidents.
en.homeIntro2: Dixon's most famous prediction appeared in a 1956 Parade magazine article, where she stated that the 1960 presidential election would be won by a Democrat who would go on to be assassinated or die in office — a claim that closely matches JFK's fate. However, she also made numerous completely wrong predictions, including World War III, the Soviet Union landing on the moon first, and more.
en.homeIntro3: Mathematician John Allen Paulos coined the term 'Jeane Dixon effect' to describe how people tend to remember the few predictions that come true while ignoring the many that don't. This cognitive bias concept remains widely cited today. This site compiles her most notable predictions with verification for each.
en.coreQuote: "The 1960 election will be won by a Democrat, and he will be assassinated or die in office." — Parade magazine, 1956
keyPointsList: 1956 —— Published the JFK-related prediction in Parade magazine
1969 —— Ruth Montgomery published 'A Gift of Prophecy', Dixon's fame peaked
1986 —— John Allen Paulos coined the 'Jeane Dixon effect'
sourceList: Prophecies compiled from Dixon's published newspaper columns, books, and biographies (Wikipedia)
The 1956 Parade magazine prediction is the core primary source (Snopes fact-check)
'Jeane Dixon effect' coined by mathematician John Allen Paulos in his 1988 book 'Innumeracy' (Wikipedia: Jeane Dixon effect)
Verification based on public news reports and historical records
Site icon: crystal ball symbol — Dixon was famous for her image of gazing into a crystal ball
